frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Protracted decision evidence integrated across time toward a final judgment.

frameworks.name: confirmation bias
frameworks.one_line: Selective down-weighting of post-choice evidence inconsistent with the prior choice.

core_question: How does making an intermittent binary choice mid-stream alter the temporal weighting of subsequent decision evidence?
key_assumptions: Decisions arise from accumulation of evidence over an extended stream
A motor-only response can serve as a choice-free control
Temporal weighting profiles can be recovered from final continuous estimates
manipulations: Intermittent response type: binary choice vs choice-independent motor response
Timing of intermittent response within the evidence stream
key_findings: Intermittent choice reduced sensitivity of subsequent evidence
Intermittent choice flipped the relative weighting of early vs late evidence in the final estimate
Individual non-selective sensitivity reduction predicted selective down-weighting of choice-inconsistent evidence
interpretations: Committing to an intermittent choice reshapes downstream evidence integration
Choice-induced overall sensitivity loss is linked to a confirmation-bias-like selective filter
